questions.question_text: How many credit hours of pre-licensing education must an associate broker candidate complete in New Mexico?
questions.explanation: Associate broker candidates must complete 90 credit hours divided into three 30-hour courses: Real Estate Principles and Practice, Real Estate Law, and Broker Basics.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed their NM pre-licensing courses 4 years ago and now wants to take the exam. What must they do?
questions.correct_answer: Retake the courses because they expired
distractors: Take the exam immediately without issue
Complete 12 hours of bridge courses only
Request a one-year extension from NMREC
questions.explanation: Pre-licensing courses must be completed within 3 years of the exam application. After 3 years, the courses expire and must be retaken.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain an associate broker license in New Mexico?
questions.explanation: While there is no age minimum to take the exam, a candidate must be at least 18 years old to actually obtain the associate broker license.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is required before an associate broker license can be activated?
distractors: Registering a trade name with NMREC
questions.explanation: Before activation, associate brokers must obtain Errors and Omissions insurance and have the certificate on file with NMREC. Trade name registration is a qualifying broker requirement.

questions.question_text: Which pre-licensing course is NOT part of the NM associate broker 90-hour requirement?
questions.explanation: The 90-hour associate broker program includes Principles and Practice (30 hrs), Real Estate Law (30 hrs), and Broker Basics (30 hrs). Brokerage Office Administration is the additional 30 hours required for the qualifying broker upgrade (120 total).

questions.question_text: To upgrade to qualifying broker, an associate broker must have how many years of active experience?
questions.explanation: To become a qualifying broker who supervises associate brokers, NM requires 4 years of active associate broker experience during the 60-month period immediately preceding the application (16.61.3.10 NMAC). A qualifying broker license that will not supervise other brokers requires only 2 years, but that broker must document 4 years of combined experience before becoming eligible to supervise.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker must complete a total of how many credit hours of pre-licensing education?
questions.explanation: Qualifying brokers need 120 total hours — the 90-hour associate broker program plus a 30-hour Brokerage Office Administration course.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed qualifying broker opens a brokerage. Which of the following is required?
questions.correct_answer: Register a trade name with NMREC
distractors: Obtain a county business permit only
Have at least two associate brokers on staff
Complete 5 years of active practice first
questions.explanation: Qualifying brokers must register a trade name with NMREC and maintain a physical office registered with the Commission before operating.

questions.question_text: An associate broker with 3 years of inactive license status applies for a QB upgrade. The candidate:
questions.correct_answer: Does not qualify due to inactive experience
distractors: Qualifies if they pass the QB exam
Qualifies if they complete extra CE hours
questions.explanation: Qualifying broker experience must be active practice within the 60-month period immediately preceding the application; inactive license time does not count toward the requirement (16.61.3.10 NMAC).

questions.question_text: What additional course must an associate broker complete to become a qualifying broker?
questions.explanation: Beyond the 90-hour AB curriculum, qualifying broker candidates must complete a 30-hour Brokerage Office Administration course, bringing their total to 120 hours.

questions.question_text: How many hours of CE must an NM associate broker complete per 3-year renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Associate brokers must complete 36 hours of continuing education per 3-year cycle, including annual Core courses, Ethics, and a Core Elective.

questions.question_text: Which CE component must an associate broker complete every year, not just once per cycle?
questions.explanation: The 4-hour NMREC Core Course must be completed annually (12 total over 3 years). Ethics (4 hrs) and Core Elective (4 hrs) are each required only once per cycle.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker must complete how many CE hours per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Qualifying brokers must complete 42 hours per 3-year cycle (vs. 36 for associate brokers), which includes a QB Refresher course. QBs must also attend one NMREC meeting or hearing.

questions.question_text: The standard license renewal fee for a NM broker is:
questions.explanation: The standard renewal fee is $270 for the 3-year cycle. Late renewal incurs a $540 fee (double the standard) plus a $30 Recovery Fund fee.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker in NM must attend which event once per 3-year renewal cycle in addition to CE?
questions.correct_answer: An NMREC meeting or disciplinary hearing
distractors: A state legislature session on real estate
questions.explanation: Qualifying brokers must attend one NMREC meeting, rules hearing, or disciplinary hearing for at least 3 hours (or until it ends) once per renewal cycle.

questions.question_text: New associate brokers in NM must complete how many hours of post-license education in their first year?
questions.explanation: New associate brokers must complete 30 hours of post-licensing education within their first year of licensure, such as the New Broker Business Practices Course or CCIM 101.

questions.question_text: A broker's E&O insurance lapses and they continue practicing. What consequence can NMREC impose?
distractors: A warning letter with 90-day grace period
No consequence until the next renewal
questions.explanation: E&O insurance must be maintained continuously while the license is active. Failure to maintain coverage is grounds for license inactivation and potential discipline.

questions.question_text: Where must a broker's E&O insurance certificate be kept on file?
distractors: With the broker's qualifying broker only
questions.explanation: All active brokers must have their E&O insurance certificate on file with NMREC. This allows the Commission to verify coverage at any time.

questions.question_text: Which statement about NM E&O insurance is correct?
questions.correct_answer: Both associate and qualifying brokers need it
distractors: Only brokers handling trust funds need it
It is optional but recommended by NMREC
questions.explanation: New Mexico requires Errors and Omissions insurance for all active brokers — both associate brokers and qualifying brokers. It is mandatory, not optional.

questions.question_text: A new associate broker obtained their license on March 1. By what date must they complete post-license education?
distractors: By December 31 of the same year
By the end of the first renewal cycle
questions.explanation: Post-license education (30 hours) must be completed within the first year of licensure. A license issued March 1 means the deadline is approximately one year later.
